own_review.issue: Static firstDeploymentClassLoader is set on the first continuous-testing run and never cleared. Every later run loads FacadeClassLoader through it, so the first deployment classloader (and, via the new QuarkusClassLoader.curatedApplication back-reference, the first CuratedApplication and everything reachable from it) is pinned for the JVM lifetime; if that loader is closed on app restart, later loadClass calls run against a closed loader.

getStartupAction creates a local 'shutdownTasks = new HashSet()' that makeCuratedApplication populates with curatedApplication::close, then discards the collection without ever running the tasks - newly created curated applications are never closed (resource/classloader leak). Same discard pattern in FacadeClassLoader.getOrCreateCuratedApplication (lines 531-537).

Potential infinite recursion: getTestClassesLocation(Class) for protocol 'quarkus' delegates to getTestClassesLocation(Class, CuratedApplication), whose final fallback calls getTestClassesLocation(Class) again with unchanged state - StackOverflowError whenever the test class is not found in any workspace output tree while loaded by a QuarkusClassLoader.

evaluateExecutionCondition catches Exception|ServiceConfigurationError, logs the underlying exception only at debug level, and throws a new IllegalStateException WITHOUT chaining the cause - the real config failure is invisible in test reports.

Static curatedApplications HashMap: (a) never cleared or closed in close(), entries accumulate for the JVM lifetime; (b) the constructor unconditionally puts the passed curatedApplication (null in the single-arg constructor used by CustomLauncherInterceptor) under the no-profile key, clobbering a previously cached, still-open application without closing it; (c) plain HashMap with no synchronization although classloaders must be thread-safe and Gradle can create several instances.

Hard-coded 'hackPendingDeferredDevServiceStart = true' makes tcpServer.stop() unreachable - the H2 TCP server is deliberately never shut down (acknowledged port leak shipping to main, pending #45785/#45786).

Behavior regression: @TestProfile on @Nested classes now throws TestInstantiationException('@Nested tests may not contain @TestProfile annotations.'); previously supported - the covering tests were deleted (WithTestProfileAndNestedTest) or @Disabled (QuarkusTestNestedWithTestProfileTestCase.ModernEnglishCase). Also the new restart condition '(state == null && !failedBoot) || (runningQuarkusApplication != null && isNewApplication)' no longer restarts when state exists but runningQuarkusApplication is null, and drops the explicit testResourcesRequireReload check.

Self-contradictory null-superclass guard: the loop condition was changed to tolerate current == null (per the @interface comment), but the new inner 'if (current == null) throw RuntimeException(Internal error...)' fires first, so interfaces/@interface still fail - the null tolerance in the condition is dead code.

Extends SmallRyeConfigProviderResolver but stores the previous resolver instance in an unused field (dead delegate); registers config against the current TCCL rather than the classLoader it built config for; FacadeClassLoader installs a fresh instance via ConfigProviderResolver.setInstance per runtime classloader creation, discarding registrations held by the previous global instance.

origCl is overwritten on every interception whose TCCL is not the facade loader, so close() may restore a later, wrong classloader; the 'only one FacadeClassLoader' intent is per-instance only and not enforced across the multiple interceptor instances Gradle creates.

Sorting by o.getTestClass().getClassLoader().getName() can NPE: getClassLoader() is null for bootstrap-loaded classes and getName() is null for unnamed loaders (plain URLClassLoader); Comparator.comparing does not tolerate null keys.

clone() reconstructs state by sharing the same shutdownHook Thread, clearCallbacks Runnable and Closeables across classloaders, supports only two hard-coded class names (IntegrationTestExtensionState, a real subclass, would hit UnsupportedOperationException), and each getState call on a cross-classloader object produces a fresh clone, so mutations (setTestFailed, close-induced shutdownHook=null) never propagate back to the stored original.

~100 lines of project-root/path-resolution and bootstrap-builder logic copy-pasted from AppMakerHelper.makeCuratedApplication (author TODO admits it); AppMakerHelper also carries dead copied static fields (quarkusTestMethodContextClass, hasPerTestResources, testMethodInvokers) and a non-thread-safe configCleanup instance field.

points.point: H2 hackPendingDeferredDevServiceStart hard-coded true disables shutdown, port leak
points.note: Verified: tcpServer.stop() unreachable.

points.point: AbstractJvmQuarkusTestExtension.getCuratedApplication unchecked QuarkusClassLoader cast, no guard, opaque CCE
points.note: Verified at line 101; contrast with getClassLoaderFromTestClass is accurate.

points.point: AppMakerHelper duplicates path-resolution logic with QuarkusMainTestExtension.getCuratedApplication
points.note: Verified ~100-line duplication (direction slightly mischaracterized - both are new in this PR - but the finding stands).

points.point: TestResourceManager raw cast (Class<? extends QuarkusTestResourceLifecycleManager>) entry.clazz unsafe across classloaders
points.note: Real type-safety erosion, but the unchecked cast itself cannot fail at that line (erasure); speculative downstream risk, hedged 'consider'.

points.point: QuarkusTestExtensionState.clone called; warns Thread shutdownHook and Closeable testResourceManager must not be naively shared
points.note: Hedged due to truncation, but the actual implementation does share exactly those references - the flagged risk is real.

points.point: TODO 'do much less of this' in doJavaStart suggests redundant startup work - verify
points.note: Restates author TODO, no issue identified.

points.point: StartupActionImpl TODO 'recreations in JUnitTestRunner for dev mode' known gap
points.note: Restates author TODO.

points.point: ClassCoercingTestProfile.testResources TODO 'not safe' - track as follow-up
points.note: Restates author-acknowledged limitation.

points.point: Praise for @Disabled with descriptive message on ModernEnglishCase
points.note: Positive observation; does surface the @Nested+@TestProfile regression, but frames it as good practice rather than a user-facing break.

points.point: QuarkusClassLoader curatedApplication/startupAction back-references create bidirectional refs; check GC of classloaders
points.note: Verified: this is exactly the retention mechanism behind the firstDeploymentClassLoader leak; hedged but correct and material.

missed: Never-cleared static firstDeploymentClassLoader in JunitTestRunner pinning first deployment CL + CuratedApplication (despite v5 gesturing at GC risk of back-references, the concrete leak site was not found)
Discarded shutdownTasks in AppMakerHelper.getStartupAction / FacadeClassLoader.getOrCreateCuratedApplication - curated apps never closed
Potential infinite recursion PathTestHelper.getTestClassesLocation (quarkus protocol <-> 2-arg fallback)
FacadeClassLoader static curatedApplications map never cleared/closed and null-overwrite of no-profile key (file was beyond the truncation, but rubric counts it)
Human's point: questioning the pre-discovery classloader-swap design in FacadeClassLoader (bytecode-added JUnit annotations rationale)

points.point: H2 hackPendingDeferredDevServiceStart port leak should not be merged
points.note: Verified.

points.point: DerbyDevServicesProcessor shares the same in-memory TCP server architecture but lacks the equivalent workaround
points.note: Verified: Derby starts a NetworkServerControl TCP server on a port with a close() that shuts it down, no hack guard - accurate and genuinely additive cross-component insight.

points.point: ParentLastURLClassLoader does not override loadClass(name, resolve); resolved-variant callers bypass parent-last and get parent-first behavior
points.note: The missing override is true, but the described failure mode is wrong: super was constructed with parent=null, so the two-arg path is bootstrap-then-self (it would merely skip the fallback parent), and ClassLoader.loadClass(String) delegates with resolve=false; external callers cannot invoke the protected variant.

points.point: Static curatedApplications is a plain HashMap; single-threaded-discovery invariant unenforced under parallel execution
points.note: Verified: static mutable HashMap, unsynchronized loadClass, multiple instances possible (Gradle).

points.point: Static curatedApplications never cleared - resource/classloader leak in long-running JVMs
points.note: Verified: close() clears runtimeClassLoaders but not the static map; entries are also never closed.

points.point: clone() shares shutdownHook Thread, clearCallbacks and testResourceManager across classloaders; duplicate removeShutdownHook races; clearCallbacks may capture foreign-CL types
points.note: Verified sharing; IllegalStateException claim slightly overstated (removeShutdownHook returns false rather than throwing unless shutdown in progress) but the shared-mutable-state hazard is real.

points.point: clone() hardcoded FQN string comparison; other subclasses hit UnsupportedOperationException
points.note: Verified: IntegrationTestExtensionState is a real third subclass that would land in the else branch.

points.point: Typo 'Internal errror' in ConfigRecorder
points.note: Real but cosmetic.

points.point: JunitTestRunner Builder.setTestApplication now ignored per TODO; deprecate or remove
points.note: Restates author TODO.

points.point: TCCL-switching logic in evaluateExecutionCondition complex and fragile; consider simpler hook
points.note: Vague design commentary echoing the author's own comment; does not identify the concrete cause-swallowing defect in the same method.

points.point: AppMakerHelper TEST_LOCATION/TEST_CLASS/TEST_PROFILE constants duplicated
points.note: Minor, restates 'TODO Copied' comment.

points.point: FacadeClassLoader.close() does not close runtimeClassLoaders; implicit contract should be documented/tested
points.note: Restates the code comment; the adjacent real bug (curated apps never closed via discarded shutdownTasks) is not identified.

missed: Never-cleared static firstDeploymentClassLoader in JunitTestRunner pinning first deployment CL + CuratedApplication
Cause-swallowing IllegalStateException in evaluateExecutionCondition (v7 circled the method but missed the defect)
Human's point: questioning the pre-discovery classloader-swap design in FacadeClassLoader
exceeds: Derby parity gap (same architecture, no workaround)
clone() hardcoded FQN fragility (real subclass exists)

qualitative: Both candidates are clean on fabrications - every absence claim I checked (Derby lacking the workaround, missing loadClass(name,resolve) override, never-cleared static map, close() not closing runtime classloaders) verified true against the full PR branch. v7 is the stronger review: having git tools, it actually read FacadeClassLoader and QuarkusTestExtensionState and converted them into five verified findings (including the static curatedApplications leak, which overlaps my own top-tier list, and a genuinely additive Derby cross-component observation), while v5, blinded by truncation, leaned on hedged 'ensure/verify' phrasing and TODO restatements - though its unchecked-cast and back-reference-GC points were real and well-aimed. Both missed the deepest defects that require tracing across files: the never-cleared firstDeploymentClassLoader GC-root chain, the discarded shutdownTasks, the PathTestHelper mutual recursion, the swallowed cause in evaluateExecutionCondition, and the human's design question about pre-discovery class loading, so heavy missed-penalties pull both nets near zero. v7's only mechanically wrong reasoning (the ParentLastURLClassLoader resolve-variant claim) was graded trivial rather than wrong since the flagged gap itself exists.
